creator: roam@ringlet.net
text:
On Mon, Jun 07, 2010 at 09:50:04AM +0000, Terry Poulin wrote:
>  Currently installed is libelf-0.8.13_1, which I built after updating
>  gettext - by way of doing a pushd /usr/ports/devel/libelf; make build
>  deinstall reinstall. What the version installed prior to upgrading
>  gettext via portmaster (see pr), according to my typescript: was
>  libelf-0.8.13 and portmaster did see it as a GCC dependency, but
>  apparently did not upgrade it.
>  
>  If libelf was bumped to match the gettext upgrade, portmaster sure as
>  heck didn't update it. The option flags used with portmaster were
>  focused on preventing unnecessary make clean/distclean runs (e.g. to
>  speed up ports like qt4-*), otherwise it was a fairly normal
>  portmaster -r gettext operation. (The -r port means rebuild port and
>  all deps).

Unfortunately, I'm not sure what exactly is "to blame" here, since
the CVS logs show that libelf's PORTVERSION was indeed bumped as part of
(and slightly after) the gettext update:

  http://cvsweb.FreeBSD.org/ports/devel/gettext/Makefile#rev1.86
  - at Mon May 31 01:51:54 2010 UTC

  http://cvsweb.FreeBSD.org/ports/devel/libelf/Makefile#rev1.26
  - at on May 31 01:58:17 2010 UTC

I just don't know what more could have been done here :)
Thanks for reporting this.. but I don't think that there is anything
more to be done at this point.

G'luck,
Peter

-- 
Peter Pentchev	roam@ringlet.net    roam@space.bg    roam@FreeBSD.org
PGP key:	http://people.FreeBSD.org/~roam/roam.key.asc
